name: Zucchini Panzotti
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch Italian parsley, finely chopped (about 1/4 cup)
1/4 pound Cacio di Roma (or other semi-soft sheep's milk cheese)
recipeInstructions:
TO MAKE PANZOTTI:
1. Rinse and dry the zucchini and trim both ends. Slice into 1/16-inch rounds and set aside.
2. In a 10- to 12-inch saute pan, heat olive oil until smoking. Add onion and garlic and cook over medium heat until lightly browned and softened, about 8 to 10 minutes.
3. Add zucchini and oregano leaves and cook until very soft, about 7 to 8 minutes. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
4. In a mixing bowl, stir together the coated zucchini mixture, egg, ricotta, Pecorino, nutmeg, and parsley until well mixed. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
5. Roll out pasta into sheets on the second thinnest setting and cut into 3-inch rounds using a water glass or pasta cutter. Place about 1/2 tablespoon of zucchini mixture into each and fold into a half moon.
6. Continue rolling until all the pasta is used.
7. Bring 6 quarts of water to boil and add 2 tablespoons salt. Drop the panzotti into the boiling water and cook until tender, about 3 to 4 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, melt butter in a 12- to 14-inch saute pan and add sage leaves. Drain the pasta in a colander and pour it into the butter mixture. Toss to coat well and pour into a heated pasta dish.
9. Shave Cacio over the pasta using a vegetable peeler and serve.
Yield: 4 servings
